text:
In the quiet town of Oakwood sat a small, well-loved library named Laurel. It was no ordinary place; its walls whispered tales through forgotten books and the creaky wooden floors echoed with laughter.

There lived a young girl named Lily. She wasn't famous or rich; she lived simply, dreaming big. There was a magical thing about Laurel—it seemed to know when Lily was feeling down. On cloudy days, it wore a soft gray raincoat. On sunny days, it wore vibrant red.

One evening, as the leaves turned gold, something odd happened. The raincoat came alive and danced around the library, calling out, "Lily! Let’s dive into something extraordinary!"

She shrugged, curious. Attracted by the sound, she picked up a forgotten volume about mermaids. As she read, her eyes widened. Suddenly, the rainbow flew into the book stand, transforming into a dazzling sea creature. Lily laughed until her sides hurt. That laughter woke her aunt, who
